title: Play it as it lies: the first commandment
microFact: Golf's core rule: play the ball as it lies, play the course as you find it. Everything else is exceptions.
lesson:
The modern Rules of Golf (rewritten in 2019, governed by the R&A and USGA) all flow from one principle: PLAY THE BALL AS IT LIES. Bad lie in a divot? Play it. Behind a tree? Play it or take penalty relief.

You may not improve your lie: no pressing grass down behind the ball, no bending branches, no nudging the ball to a nicer tuft. Moving your ball accidentally (other than on the green or while searching) usually costs a stroke — replace it.

What you MAY do: remove LOOSE IMPEDIMENTS (leaves, stones, twigs — natural objects) anywhere, carefully, and MOVABLE OBSTRUCTIONS (rakes, bottles — artificial objects). If the ball moves while removing a loose impediment (off the green), that's a penalty.

Why this matters socially: quietly playing a terrible lie without complaint is one of the most respected behaviors in golf. Nudgers get spotted, and the label never washes off.

quiz.question: Your ball sits against a fallen leaf and a candy wrapper. What can you remove penalty-free?
options: Neither — play it as it lies
quiz.explanation: Leaves are loose impediments, wrappers are movable obstructions — both removable. Just don't move the ball doing it.
